steps.title: Use vertex form since the maximum is given
steps.content: The problem tells us the maximum daylight is 13.84 hours at $t = 3$ months. For a quadratic with a known vertex $(h, k)$, use vertex form: $$d(t) = a(t - h)^2 + k$$ where $(h, k) = (3, 13.84)$. This gives us: $$d(t) = a(t - 3)^2 + 13.84$$

steps.title: Substitute the second point to find a
Find daylight on March 1
steps.content: Now evaluate $d(0)$ to find the daylight on March 1: $$d(0) = -0.2(0 - 3)^2 + 13.84$$ $$d(0) = -0.2(9) + 13.84$$ $$d(0) = -1.8 + 13.84$$ $$d(0) = 12.04$$ The estimated length of daylight on March 1 is 12.04 hours.
